harness.en: a set of straps used to control an animal or keep a person safe

ex.en: The climber checked her harness before the ascent.
ex.ko: 그 등반가는 오르기 전에 안전벨트를 점검했다.

harness.en: control and use a natural force or resource for a particular purpose

ex.en: Wind farms harness the power of the wind.

hasty.en: done too quickly, without enough thought or care

ex.en: He made a hasty decision and regretted it later.
ex.ko: 그는 성급한 결정을 내렸고 나중에 후회했다.

hatch.en: (of a young bird, insect, or other animal) come out of an egg

ex.en: The chicks hatched after three weeks.

hatch.en: a small covered opening in a floor, wall, or door of a ship, aircraft, or building

ex.en: He climbed through the hatch onto the deck.
ex.ko: 그는 해치를 통해 갑판 위로 올라갔다.

hatch.en: plan or invent something, often secretly

ex.en: The two friends hatched a plan to surprise her.
ex.ko: 그 두 친구는 그녀를 놀라게 할 계획을 꾸몄다.

hatred.en: a very strong feeling of dislike for someone or something

ex.en: Their rivalry grew into open hatred.

haunt.en: if a memory or bad experience haunts you, it keeps coming back into your mind and upsets you

ex.en: The image of the accident still haunts him.
ex.ko: 그 사고의 장면이 아직도 그를 계속 괴롭힌다.

haunt.en: (of a ghost) appear in a place regularly

ex.en: People say the old house is haunted by a ghost.
ex.ko: 사람들은 그 낡은 집에 귀신이 출몰한다고 말한다.

haunt.en: a place that someone visits often

ex.en: This café is a favorite haunt of local writers.
ex.ko: 이 카페는 지역 작가들이 자주 찾는 아지트다.

haven.en: a place that is safe and peaceful

ex.en: The garden became a haven from the noisy city.
ex.ko: 그 정원은 시끄러운 도시로부터의 안식처가 되었다.

hazard.en: something that could cause danger or harm

ex.en: Icy roads are a serious hazard for drivers.

hazard.en: say something even though it might be wrong

ex.en: I'll hazard a guess that she's running late.
ex.ko: 그녀가 늦고 있을 거라고 추측해 볼게.

headline.en: the title of a newspaper article, printed in large letters

ex.en: The headline announced the election results.

heal.en: become healthy again after an injury or illness, or make someone healthy again

ex.en: The wound healed quickly.

healing.en: helping to cure an illness or injury

ex.en: Aloe vera has healing properties for the skin.

healing.en: the process of becoming healthy again after an illness or injury

ex.en: Healing takes time after surgery.
Her hearing has gotten worse with age.

hearing.en: a formal meeting where evidence is presented and a decision is made, especially in a court

ex.en: The senator testified at the hearing.

heart attack.en: a sudden serious medical problem in which the heart stops working normally

ex.en: He suffered a heart attack at the age of 60.

hearted.en: used after an adjective to describe someone's character or nature

ex.en: She is warm-hearted and always willing to help.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마음이 따뜻해서 늘 기꺼이 도와준다.

hearty.en: warm and friendly, showing sincere feeling

ex.en: They gave us a hearty welcome.

hearty.en: (of food) large in amount and satisfying

ex.en: We had a hearty breakfast before the hike.
ex.ko: 우리는 하이킹 전에 푸짐한 아침을 먹었다.

heater.en: a device that produces heat to warm a room or space

ex.en: Turn on the heater, it's freezing in here.
ex.ko: 히터 좀 켜, 여기 너무 춥다.

heaven.cue: 신이 사는 곳, 죽은 후 가는 곳
heaven.en: the place where, according to some religions, good people go after death

ex.en: She believes her grandmother is in heaven now.
ex.ko: 그녀는 할머니가 지금 천국에 계신다고 믿는다.

heavenly.en: relating to heaven or the sky

ex.en: They spoke of heavenly rewards after death.
ex.ko: 그들은 죽음 이후의 천국의 보상에 대해 이야기했다.

heel.en: the back part of the foot, below the ankle

ex.en: She has a blister on her heel.

heel.en: the raised part of a shoe under the back of the foot

ex.en: She can't walk in high heels.

height.en: how tall a person or thing is

ex.en: What is your height in centimeters?

height.en: the point when something is at its strongest, most successful, or most extreme

ex.en: The band was at the height of its fame in the 1990s.

helmet.en: a hard hat worn to protect the head

ex.en: Always wear a helmet when riding a bike.
ex.ko: 자전거를 탈 때는 항상 헬멧을 착용해라.

helpless.cue: 스스로 할 수 있는 게 없는
helpless.en: unable to help or protect yourself; without power

ex.en: The baby bird looked helpless on the ground.
ex.ko: 그 아기 새는 땅에서 무력해 보였다.

herd.en: a large group of animals of the same kind that live and feed together

ex.en: A herd of cows grazed in the field.
ex.ko: 소 떼가 들판에서 풀을 뜯고 있었다.

herd.en: make people or animals move together in a particular direction

ex.en: The guards herded the tourists into the bus.

hesitate.en: pause before doing or deciding something because you are unsure

ex.en: Don't hesitate to call me if you need help.

hidden.en: kept out of sight or not easily noticed

ex.en: There's a hidden camera in the room.

highlight.en: the most interesting or exciting part of something

ex.en: The highlight of the trip was visiting the waterfall.
ex.ko: 그 여행의 하이라이트는 폭포를 방문한 것이었다.

highlight.en: make something easy to notice or show that it is important

ex.en: The report highlights the need for more funding.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 추가 자금의 필요성을 강조한다.

ex.en: She is a highly skilled surgeon.

hip.en: the part of the body between the waist and the top of the leg

ex.en: She broke her hip in the fall.
That new café is really hip.
The company hired ten new employees.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 신입 사원 열 명을 고용했다.

hire.en: pay to use something for a short time (British English)

ex.en: We hired a car for the weekend.

historian.en: a person who studies or writes about history

ex.en: The historian wrote a book about ancient Rome.
ex.ko: 그 역사학자는 고대 로마에 관한 책을 썼다.

historic.en: important enough to be remembered as part of history

ex.en: They signed a historic peace agreement.

historical.en: relating to the study of history or to past events

ex.en: The museum has a large historical collection.
ex.ko: 그 박물관에는 방대한 역사 유물 컬렉션이 있다.

holy.en: connected with God or a particular religion; sacred

ex.en: Mecca is a holy city for Muslims.

homeless.en: having no home or place to live

ex.en: The charity helps homeless people find shelter.
ex.ko: 그 자선단체는 노숙자들이 쉼터를 찾도록 돕는다.

homeless.en: people who have no home (used with 'the')

ex.en: The city opened a new shelter for the homeless.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 노숙자들을 위한 새 쉼터를 열었다.

honest.en: telling the truth and not lying or cheating

ex.en: Be honest with me about what happened.
ex.ko: 무슨 일이 있었는지 나에게 정직하게 말해줘.

honestly.en: used to say what you really think or feel

ex.en: Honestly, I don't think this plan will work.
ex.ko: 솔직히, 나는 이 계획이 성공할 거라고 생각하지 않아.

ex.en: Honesty is the most important value in our family.
ex.ko: 정직함은 우리 가족에서 가장 중요한 가치다.

honeymoon.en: a vacation taken by a couple right after their wedding

ex.en: They went to Hawaii for their honeymoon.

hop.cue: 짧게 한 번 또는 여러 번 뛰다
hop.en: jump a short distance, especially on one foot or with your feet together

ex.en: The rabbit hopped across the yard.

hopeful.en: feeling or showing hope that something good will happen

ex.en: I'm hopeful that things will get better soon.
ex.ko: 나는 상황이 곧 나아질 거라고 기대하고 있어.

hopefully.en: used to say what you hope will happen

ex.en: Hopefully, the weather will be nice for the trip.
ex.ko: 부디 여행 갈 때 날씨가 좋았으면 좋겠다.

hopeless.en: having no hope of success or improvement

ex.en: The situation seemed completely hopeless.
I'm hopeless at cooking.

horizon.en: the line where the sky seems to meet the land or sea

ex.en: The sun disappeared below the horizon.

horn.en: a device in a vehicle that makes a loud warning sound

ex.en: The driver honked the horn to warn the cyclist.
ex.ko: 운전자는 자전거 탄 사람에게 경고하려고 경적을 울렸다.

horn.en: one of the hard pointed parts that grow on the heads of some animals

ex.en: The bull has large horns.
ex.ko: 그 황소는 큰 뿔을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: The weather was horrible all week.

horrify.en: shock or frighten someone very much

ex.en: The news of the accident horrified everyone.
ex.ko: 그 사고 소식은 모두를 충격에 빠뜨렸다.

hostel.en: a cheap place to stay, popular with young travelers

ex.en: We stayed in a youth hostel to save money.

household.en: a group of people, often a family, who live together in one house

ex.en: The average household spends a lot on groceries.
ex.ko: 평균적인 가정은 식료품에 많은 돈을 쓴다.

housework.en: work such as cleaning that is done in the home

ex.en: They share the housework equally.

huge.en: extremely large in size or amount

ex.en: They live in a huge house.

humanity.en: all human beings considered as a group

ex.en: Climate change is a threat to humanity.

humanity.en: the quality of being kind and caring towards others

ex.en: The rescue workers showed great humanity.

humid.en: containing a lot of water vapor in the air

ex.en: Summers here are hot and humid.

humor.en: the quality of being funny, or the ability to find things funny

ex.en: He has a great sense of humor.
She gave a humorous speech at the party.

hunger.en: the feeling or state of needing food

ex.en: Many children suffer from hunger around the world.
ex.ko: 전 세계 많은 아이들이 굶주림에 시달린다.

ex.en: She has a hunger for knowledge.

hunt.en: chase and kill wild animals for food or sport

ex.en: Wolves hunt in packs.
I hunted for my keys all morning.
ex.ko: 나는 아침 내내 열쇠를 찾아 헤맸다.

ex.en: She hurriedly packed her bag and left.

hut.en: a small, simple building, often made of wood

ex.en: They spent the night in a mountain hut.

ice hockey.en: a sport played on ice in which two teams try to hit a puck into the other team's goal

ex.en: Canada is famous for ice hockey.

ice skating.en: the activity of moving around on ice wearing skates

ex.en: We went ice skating at the local rink.

identity.en: the sense of who a person is, or the qualities that make someone or something distinct

ex.en: Traveling helped her discover her own identity.
ex.ko: 여행은 그녀가 자신의 정체성을 발견하는 데 도움이 되었다.

identity.en: who someone is, especially the facts that prove it

ex.en: Please show your ID to confirm your identity.

identity card.en: an official card that shows who you are

ex.en: You must carry your identity card at all times.

idiom.cue: 단어 뜻만으로는 알 수 없는 표현
idiom.en: a group of words with a meaning that is different from the meanings of the individual words

ex.en: 'Kick the bucket' is an idiom meaning 'to die'.

idol.en: someone who is greatly admired or loved, especially a famous singer or actor

ex.en: She's been a pop idol since she was a teenager.

idol.en: a statue or object that is worshipped as a god

ex.en: The ancient temple was full of golden idols.
ex.ko: 그 고대 사원은 금으로 된 신상들로 가득했다.

ignore.en: pay no attention to someone or something on purpose

ex.en: She ignored his phone calls all day.
ex.ko: 그녀는 하루 종일 그의 전화를 무시했다.

illegally.en: in a way that is against the law

ex.en: He was caught parking illegally.

illness.en: a disease or period of being sick

ex.en: She missed school because of illness.

illuminate.en: light up a place or object

ex.en: Fireworks illuminated the night sky.

illuminate.en: help to explain or make something clearer

ex.en: Her research illuminates how children learn language.
ex.ko: 그녀의 연구는 아이들이 언어를 배우는 방식을 밝혀준다.

imaginary.en: existing only in someone's imagination; not real

ex.en: Many children have imaginary friends.
ex.ko: 많은 아이들이 상상 속 친구를 가지고 있다.

imitate.en: copy the way someone speaks, moves, or behaves

ex.en: The comedian imitated the president's voice perfectly.
ex.ko: 그 코미디언은 대통령의 목소리를 완벽하게 흉내냈다.

immediate.en: happening or done without any delay

ex.en: The medicine had an immediate effect.
Only immediate family attended the funeral.
Please call me immediately if anything happens.

immigrate.en: come to live permanently in a country that is not your own

ex.en: Her grandparents immigrated to the US in the 1950s.

immigration.en: the act of coming to live permanently in a foreign country

ex.en: The government introduced new immigration policies.

impair.en: damage something or make it weaker or worse

ex.en: Drinking alcohol impairs your judgment.

impression.en: the way someone or something seems to you, or the effect they have on your opinion

ex.en: He made a good first impression at the interview.

impressive.en: making you feel admiration because of its size, quality, or skill

ex.en: Her performance was truly impressive.
It's improper to talk during the ceremony.

improvement.en: a change that makes something better

ex.en: There has been a big improvement in her English.
ex.ko: 그녀의 영어 실력에 큰 향상이 있었다.

incident.cue: 일어난 일, 특히 나쁘거나 특이한 일
incident.en: something that happens, especially something unusual or unpleasant

ex.en: Police are investigating the incident.

including.en: used to show that someone or something is part of a larger group being mentioned

ex.en: Five people were injured, including two children.
ex.ko: 두 명의 아이를 포함해 다섯 명이 다쳤다.

income.en: the money that a person or organization earns or receives

ex.en: Their household income increased last year.

inconvenient.en: causing problems or difficulty, especially at a bad time

ex.en: Is it an inconvenient time to call?
Two of his answers were incorrect.

increasingly.en: more and more as time passes

ex.en: Online shopping is becoming increasingly popular.
ex.ko: 온라인 쇼핑은 점점 더 인기가 많아지고 있다.

incredible.en: so good, large, or surprising that it is hard to believe

ex.en: She has an incredible talent for music.
ex.ko: 그녀는 믿을 수 없을 정도의 음악적 재능을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: The test was incredibly difficult.
ex.ko: 그 시험은 믿을 수 없을 정도로 어려웠다.

indefinite article.cue: 특정하지 않은 것을 가리킬 때 쓰는 관사
indefinite article.en: the words 'a' or 'an' in English, used before a noun that is not specific

ex.en: 'A' and 'an' are indefinite articles.

independent.en: not needing help or support from other people

ex.en: She became financially independent after college.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대학 졸업 후 경제적으로 독립적이 되었다.

indirect.en: not going straight to the point, or not directly caused

ex.en: He gave an indirect answer to avoid the question.
ex.ko: 그는 질문을 피하려고 간접적인 답을 했다.

indirectly.en: in a way that is not direct

ex.en: The scandal indirectly affected the whole company.
ex.ko: 그 스캔들은 회사 전체에 간접적으로 영향을 미쳤다.

individual.en: a single person, considered separately from a group

ex.en: Every individual has the right to vote.

individual.en: relating to a single person or thing, rather than a group

ex.en: The teacher gave each student individual attention.
ex.ko: 선생님은 각 학생에게 개별적으로 관심을 기울였다.

ex.en: It's raining, so let's stay indoors.
This is an industrial area with many factories.

industry.en: the companies and activities involved in producing goods or services

ex.en: The tourism industry is important to this country.

inevitable.en: certain to happen and impossible to avoid

ex.en: Change is inevitable in any growing business.

infection.en: an illness caused by bacteria or a virus entering the body

ex.en: She developed an ear infection.

infinitive.en: the basic form of a verb, usually used with 'to' (e.g. 'to go')

ex.en: 'To go' is the infinitive form of the verb 'go'.

inform.en: tell someone about a fact or situation

ex.en: Please inform us of any changes to your address.
ex.ko: 주소 변경 사항이 있으면 저희에게 알려주세요.

ex.en: The documentary was very informative.

ingredient.en: one of the foods or substances used to make a dish

ex.en: Flour, sugar, and eggs are the main ingredients.

inhabitant.en: a person who lives in a particular place

ex.en: The city has over a million inhabitants.
ex.ko: 그 도시에는 백만 명이 넘는 주민이 산다.

ex.en: She inhaled deeply and closed her eyes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 숨을 깊이 들이쉬고 눈을 감았다.

ex.en: My initial reaction was surprise.

initial.en: the first letter of a name

ex.en: He signed the document with his initials.
Initially, I was nervous about the new job.

inject.en: put a liquid, especially a drug, into someone's body using a needle

ex.en: The nurse injected the vaccine into his arm.

inject.en: add a new quality or amount of something to a situation

ex.en: The government injected more money into the economy.
ex.ko: 정부는 경제에 더 많은 돈을 투입했다.

injury.en: physical harm or damage to the body

ex.en: He suffered a serious injury during the game.
ex.ko: 그는 경기 중 심각한 부상을 입었다.

ink.en: a colored liquid used for writing or printing

ex.en: The pen ran out of ink.

innermost.en: most private or deeply hidden, especially feelings or thoughts

ex.en: She shared her innermost thoughts with her best friend.
ex.ko: 그녀는 가장 친한 친구에게 내면 깊숙한 생각을 털어놓았다.

ex.en: The jury found him innocent.

innocent.en: having little experience of life, and therefore not expecting bad things to happen

ex.en: She has an innocent, childlike smile.
ex.ko: 그녀는 순수하고 아이 같은 미소를 가지고 있다.

innovator.en: a person who introduces new ideas or methods

ex.en: She is known as an innovator in renewable energy.
ex.ko: 그녀는 재생에너지 분야의 혁신가로 알려져 있다.

inquiry.en: a question you ask to get information

ex.en: We received several inquiries about the job posting.
ex.ko: 우리는 채용 공고에 대한 여러 문의를 받았다.

inquiry.en: an official investigation into a matter of public interest

ex.en: The government launched an inquiry into the accident.
ex.ko: 정부는 그 사고에 대한 조사를 시작했다.

insane.en: seriously mentally ill, or extremely foolish

ex.en: It would be insane to drive in this storm.
ex.ko: 이런 폭풍 속에 운전하는 것은 미친 짓일 거다.

inscribe.en: write or carve words onto a surface

ex.en: His name was inscribed on the trophy.

inscription.en: words that are carved or written on something, such as a stone or coin

ex.en: The inscription on the tombstone was hard to read.
ex.ko: 그 비석에 새겨진 글은 읽기 어려웠다.

insight.en: a clear understanding of a complicated problem or situation

ex.en: The book offers valuable insight into human behavior.
ex.ko: 그 책은 인간 행동에 대한 귀중한 통찰을 제공한다.

insist.en: demand something firmly or say something strongly, refusing to accept a different opinion

ex.en: She insisted on paying for the meal.

inspection.en: a careful examination to check that everything is correct or safe

ex.en: The car passed its safety inspection.

inspire.en: make someone want to do something or feel positive and enthusiastic

ex.en: Her story inspired many people to help others.
ex.ko: 그녀의 이야기는 많은 사람들이 다른 사람을 돕도록 영감을 주었다.

install.en: put something in place ready to be used

ex.en: We need to install new software on this computer.
ex.ko: 이 컴퓨터에 새 소프트웨어를 설치해야 한다.

instance.en: a particular example or occurrence of something

ex.en: This is just one instance of many similar problems.
ex.ko: 이것은 비슷한 문제들 중 하나의 사례일 뿐이다.

instead of.en: in place of, or as a replacement for

ex.en: I'll have tea instead of coffee.

institute.en: start or introduce a new system, rule, or law

ex.en: The school instituted a new dress code.
ex.ko: 그 학교는 새 복장 규정을 도입했다.

institute.en: an organization set up for research or education in a particular field

ex.en: She works at a research institute.

instruction.en: information or directions telling you how to do something

ex.en: Please read the instructions before assembling the desk.
The course offers instruction in three languages.
ex.ko: 그 과정은 세 개 언어로 교육을 제공한다.

instructor.en: a person who teaches a skill or subject

ex.en: Our ski instructor was very patient.
ex.ko: 우리 스키 강사는 매우 인내심이 있었다.

insurance.en: an arrangement where you pay money regularly so a company will pay for losses or damage

ex.en: He bought health insurance for his family.
ex.ko: 그는 가족을 위해 건강 보험에 가입했다.

intend.en: plan or mean to do something

ex.en: I intend to finish this project by Friday.
ex.ko: 나는 금요일까지 이 프로젝트를 끝낼 작정이다.

intense.en: very great or extreme in strength or degree

ex.en: She felt an intense pain in her back.

intensive.en: involving a lot of effort or activity in a short time

ex.en: She took an intensive English course over the summer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여름 동안 집중 영어 과정을 들었다.

intention.en: what you plan or mean to do

ex.en: It was never my intention to hurt you.
ex.ko: 너에게 상처를 줄 의도는 전혀 없었어.

ex.en: He intentionally left his phone at home.
ex.ko: 그는 일부러 휴대폰을 집에 두고 왔다.

interact.en: communicate with or react to each other

ex.en: Children learn by interacting with others.

interaction.en: communication or activity between two or more people or things

ex.en: Social interaction is important for children's development.

intermediate.en: at a middle level between beginner and advanced

ex.en: She is in the intermediate English class.
ex.ko: 그녀는 중급 영어 수업을 듣고 있다.

intermission.en: a short break in the middle of a performance or event

ex.en: We got some snacks during the intermission.
ex.ko: 우리는 중간 휴식 시간에 간식을 좀 샀다.

internal.en: existing or happening inside a place, organization, or the body

ex.en: The company conducted an internal investigation.

internationally.en: in relation to more than one country; throughout the world

ex.en: The singer is known internationally.

interrupt.en: stop someone while they are speaking or doing something

ex.en: Sorry to interrupt, but there's a phone call for you.

interval.en: the amount of time or space between two things

ex.en: Take this medicine at four-hour intervals.

interviewee.en: a person who is being interviewed

ex.en: The interviewee answered every question calmly.
ex.ko: 그 인터뷰 대상자는 모든 질문에 침착하게 답했다.

introduction.en: the act of making people known to each other for the first time

ex.en: Let me make the introductions.

introduction.en: the first part of a book or piece of writing that explains what it is about

ex.en: The introduction explains the purpose of the study.

invasion.en: an act of entering a country or place using military force

ex.en: The invasion led to a long war.

invest.en: put money into something in the hope of making a profit

ex.en: They invested in a new startup.

investigation.en: an official examination to discover the facts about something

ex.en: Police launched an investigation into the fire.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그 화재에 대한 수사를 시작했다.

invitation.en: a request for someone to come to an event

ex.en: We received an invitation to the wedding.

involuntarily.en: without intending to; without control over it

ex.en: She involuntarily flinched at the loud noise.
ex.ko: 그녀는 큰 소리에 본의 아니게 움찔했다.

involve.en: include something as a necessary part or result

ex.en: The job involves a lot of travel.

involve.en: make someone take part in something

ex.en: She likes to involve the whole team in decisions.
ex.ko: 그녀는 결정에 팀 전체를 참여시키는 것을 좋아한다.

involved.en: taking part in an activity or connected with a situation

ex.en: Many volunteers are involved in the project.
ex.ko: 많은 자원봉사자들이 그 프로젝트에 참여하고 있다.

ex.en: It's a long and involved process.

iron.en: a device with a heated flat surface used to smooth clothes

ex.en: Be careful, the iron is very hot.

iron.en: make clothes smooth using a heated iron

ex.en: He ironed his shirt before the interview.

iron.en: a strong, hard metal used to make tools and machines

ex.en: The gate is made of iron.

ironing.en: the activity of smoothing clothes with an iron, or the clothes that need this

ex.en: I still have a pile of ironing to do.
ex.ko: 나는 아직 다림질할 옷이 쌓여 있다.

hiv.en: a virus that attacks the body's immune system and can cause AIDS

ex.en: The clinic offers free HIV testing.
ex.ko: 그 병원은 무료 에이치아이브이 검사를 제공한다.
